message.content: Read S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Manage\Views\Position\_CreateOrUpdate.cshtml again, but this time focus on the form field markup. I need to see the exact HTML classes and structure used for form fields. Provide the code for 2-3 form fields as examples.

content.text: I'll read the Position form to show you the exact HTML field markup structure.

Perfect! Here are the exact HTML field markup patterns used in the Position form: ## Form Field Structure Examples ### 1. Text Input Field (Standard Pattern) ```html
@Html.LabelFor(y => y.Position) @Html.TextBoxFor(y => y.Position, new { @class = "md-input" })
``` **Key classes:** - Container: `uk-grid pt-3` (UIkit grid + padding-top) - Width wrapper: `uk-width-1-1` (full width) - Form row: `uk-form-row` - Input: `md-input` (Material Design input style) ### 2. Dropdown Field (Roles Tab) ```html
@Html.Label("Roles", new { @class = "m-0 fs-14" }) @Html.DropDownList("Roles", (IEnumerable)rolesavailables, new { @class = "md-input", id = "ddlRoles" })
``` **Key classes:** - Container: `p-0 input-group mb-3 mn-r-10` (no padding, Bootstrap input-group, margin-bottom, margin-right) - Label: `m-0 fs-14` (no margin, font-size 14px) - Dropdown: `md-input` (same as text inputs) ### 3. Dropdown with Select2 (Users Tab) ```html
@Html.Label("Users", new { @class = "m-0 fs-14" }) @Html.DropDownList("Users", (IEnumerable)usersavailables, new { @class = "md-input", id = "ddlUsers" })
``` **Key classes:** - Same as dropdown above - Enhanced with Select2 plugin via inline script ## Pattern Summary **For Building/User forms, you should use:** | Element | Classes | Notes | |---------|---------|-------| | **Outer container** | `uk-grid pt-3` or `p-0 input-group mb-3 mn-r-10` | UIkit grid or Bootstrap input-group | | **Width wrapper** | `uk-width-1-1` (full), `uk-width-1-2` (half) | UIkit responsive widths | | **Form row** | `uk-form-row` | Groups label + input | | **Label** | `m-0 fs-14` | No margin, 14px font | | **Text input** | `md-input` | Material Design style | | **Dropdown** | `md-input` | Same as text inputs | | **Hidden field** | N/A | Use `@Html.HiddenFor()` | **The two container patterns:** 1. **UIkit pattern** (General tab): `uk-grid pt-3` → `uk-width-*` → `uk-form-row` 2. **Bootstrap pattern** (Roles/Users tabs): `p-0 input-group mb-3 mn-r-10` → label + input directly Both use `md-input` for the actual form controls.
